what_it_does: Defines the diagnostics resource contract for the company fabric, mapping native Claude Code diagnostic levers (/doctor, --debug, --safe-mode/--bare, telemetry) to the company's two real surfaces (supervisor GET /health fabric health and per-session failure events on the event stream) and documenting remaining gaps.
observations: There is no diagnostics:// scheme; a diagnostic is identified by what it inspects.
The two real company diagnostic surfaces are the supervisor's live GET /health self-report and agent_sessions failure events on the event stream.
Native /doctor is interactive-host only with no headless per-session form; a per-session /doctor is planned but unverified (CC-33.3).
--debug, --safe-mode, and --bare are built into the /spawn body (CC-33.4) but live verification is pending; --bare strips mcp__company and breaks the fabric.
diagnostics.get is the same GET /health read used by fabric-config and settings.
diagnostics.watch is not a separate stream but a diagnostic lens on the existing session/event watch stream.
The state model is stateless; diagnostics observes supervisor and session lifecycles without owning its own machine.
Captured examples are synthetic because the resource status is building.
Per-turn cost/usage fields are now captured on agent_sessions.turn events (CC-20).
Error vocabulary includes diagnostics.not-exposed (501 for /doctor) and diagnostics.supervisor-down (503 refused connection).
standouts: Explicitly names the gap: the company has no per-session /doctor and no dedicated --debug stream, even though spawn flags are built.
Treats diagnostics.watch as a read lens on the existing event/session stream rather than creating a new transport.
GET /health is the single proven live diagnostic read shared across multiple resources.
Warns that --bare is isolation-only because it removes the mcp__company tool the fabric depends on.
conventions: Follows the contract-entry frontmatter pattern with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, and relates-to.
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, contract:example, and contract:error fenced blocks.
Cross-references other resources with [[resource#op]] links instead of restating shared mechanics.
Marks status as building and labels examples as synthetic until live verification.
Uses neutral, non-ranking language and avoids declaring any resource canonical.
concerns: Built /spawn debug/safe-mode/bare parameters are still under status=building with synthetic examples and pending live-verify, which may obscure readiness.
diagnostics.act mixes built spawn flags with the still-planned /doctor in one operation, creating mixed maturity signals.
The repeated warning that --bare breaks the fabric suggests a design tension between isolation and required customizations.
/doctor is described as both host-only and a possible future per-session health object, leaving scope ambiguous.
notes: A gap-aware contract that maps native Claude Code diagnostics to the company's existing supervisor health read and event-stream failure observations, marking what is built, planned, and out of scope.
questions: What is the exact transport for returning --debug output to a consumer?
How does a consumer invoke /doctor on the host versus request a per-session health object?
What are the precise event schema fields for session_failure beyond the sample?
How does OTel telemetry export relate to the fabric event stream, if at all?
What values can the permission field take in GET /health?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to map native diagnostics to company surfaces and name gaps; the file does that, but it also documents partially built spawn flags and a still-planned /doctor, so it reads as part contract and part gap register.
intention_signals: It tries to stop the UI from inventing a fictional per-session /doctor or dedicated diagnostics stream by binding each native diagnostic lever to the real fabric surfaces that already exist and marking the residual unbuilt levers so future work targets the correct seam.
novelty: It explicitly declares that there is no diagnostics:// scheme and no dedicated diagnostics stream; instead it treats diagnostics as lenses on existing supervisor health and event watch surfaces, and candidly labels native levers that are not exposed.
contribution: Provides a scope-bound diagnostics contract that prevents assumptions about unimplemented per-session /doctor and --debug streaming, documents the shared GET /health health read, and records the built-but-not-live-verified /spawn debug/safe-mode/bare parameters.
summary_for_embedding: A contract entry defining the company fabric's diagnostics resource as lenses on supervisor GET /health and the per-session event watch stream, mapping native Claude Code /doctor, --debug, --safe-mode, and --bare levers and marking per-session /doctor as unbuilt.
intention_for_embedding: To give the UI an accurate, gap-aware diagnostic contract that uses existing fabric surfaces rather than inventing new ones.
